spec_match.rationale: The migration steps and replacement sketches represent practical, standard first-party measurement practices (e.g. campaign joins on click IDs, TEE aggregate log redaction).

interactive.rationale: Features dynamic checklist toggles that instantly construct custom recommendation paragraphs and code samples, paired with an on-page browser capability check.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent index: lets users select surfaces to generate custom first-party plans covering server conversion and iframe policy adjustments.

edge_cases.rationale: Uses safe try-catch validation when verifying permissions policy states to prevent browser crashes.

references.rationale: The page contains absolutely no see-also, reference, or external link lists. It fails the repository-wide hard rule: 'Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'.

design_system.rationale: Aligns nicely with colors, font-mono variables, and two-column responsive layouts.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory ChromeStatus entry link and standard see-also footer
openQuestions.detail: To comply with the repository-wide hard rule ('Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'), we must add a 'see also' section in the footer containing a link to ChromeStatus entry 6320639375966208, alongside references to the W3C spec and companion local concept pages.

summary: An excellent, interactive planner tool that customizes migration recommendations dynamically based on checkbox inputs. However, it currently fails the repository's hard rule by omitting a direct link to the ChromeStatus feature database. Adding a standard see-also section with this link is a high priority.
